{
  "selected_image": "img_7.jpg",
  "reasoning": "This image provides a clear, professional schematic ECG trace that demonstrates the progressive widening of the QRS complex. It specifically illustrates the 'rate-dependent blockade' (use-dependence) characteristic of Class I antiarrhythmic drugs (particularly Class Ic), where the QRS interval increases as the drug binds more effectively to sodium channels over successive beats. This dynamic representation is more educational than a static wide QRS as it shows the transition from narrow to wide.",
  "description_of_selected": "A schematic ECG strip showing a sequence of heartbeats where the QRS complex progressively widens from left to right. Below the trace is a diagram illustrating the binding and unbinding kinetics of the drug, labeled 'onset kinetics' and 'rate-dependent blockade', explaining the physiological mechanism behind the observed ECG change."
}

parsed.reasoning: This image provides a clear, professional schematic ECG trace that demonstrates the progressive widening of the QRS complex. It specifically illustrates the 'rate-dependent blockade' (use-dependence) characteristic of Class I antiarrhythmic drugs (particularly Class Ic), where the QRS interval increases as the drug binds more effectively to sodium channels over successive beats. This dynamic representation is more educational than a static wide QRS as it shows the transition from narrow to wide.
parsed.description_of_selected: A schematic ECG strip showing a sequence of heartbeats where the QRS complex progressively widens from left to right. Below the trace is a diagram illustrating the binding and unbinding kinetics of the drug, labeled 'onset kinetics' and 'rate-dependent blockade', explaining the physiological mechanism behind the observed ECG change.
